body.title: superclaude .cc.N tmux naming is broken (dots->underscores): dead scan + kill-server footgun
body.description:
Surfaced 2026-07-03 while spinning up a CC.1 to test the numbering (Akien: 'that'll test the numbering for your shim'). tmux reserves '.' for session.window.pane targeting and silently stores session names with dots converted to underscores: `tmux new-session -s akiendelllinux.cc.1` creates `akiendelllinux_cc_1`, and `tmux ... -t akiendelllinux.cc.1` parses as session=akiendelllinux/window=cc/pane=1 and fails ('can't find pane: cc.1'). VERIFIED on this box's tmux. Three consequences, all in bin/superclaude:
(1) FREE-SLOT SCAN DEAD (lines 305-322 and 313-322): `tmux has-session -t ${hostname}.cc.N` with dots can never match an underscore-stored session, so the scan always reports every slot free -> the number never increments; two CCs collide on .cc.0.
(2) IN-PLACE EQUALITY NEVER HOLDS (line 338): CC_TMUX_SESSION (dotted) != `tmux display-message '#{session_name}'` (underscore) when you target the real session -> falls through to the else branch.
(3) KILL-SERVER FOOTGUN (line 353): that else branch runs `tmux kill-server`, which would nuke sudo-relay and every other session — a data-loss/uptime hazard (CP6).

**Proposed shape (needs Akien's design call on the separator):** use a dot-free tmux session name consistently (e.g. `${hostname}_cc_${N}` or `${hostname}-cc-${N}`) for ALL tmux operations (new-session, has-session, send-keys, display-message, kill-session), while keeping the logical worker identity (CC.0/CC.1, ~/.granny/available/CC.N.available.*, announce) as its own separate dotted name — the two namespaces must not be conflated. Separately: scope the else-branch cleanup to `kill-session -t <the stale name>` only; NEVER `kill-server` (CP6 — no whole-server kill).

**Affected files:** bin/superclaude (lines ~283-360). Likely HIGH inertia (load-bearing launcher) — pause for Akien pre-approval before the fix sprint.
**Design rules:** none in ticket_design_checks directly; CP6 (no unscoped destructive ops) governs the kill-server fix.
**Scope boundary:** IN — dot-free tmux session naming end-to-end in superclaude + replace kill-server with scoped kill-session. OUT — building the CC shim device (T-cc-shim-reachability-gate-backoff), Granny dispatch wiring, the logical worker-name namespace redesign.
**Test plan:** a shell/pytest harness that (a) creates two sessions via the launcher's naming and asserts distinct incremented names actually exist in `tmux ls`, (b) asserts the free-slot scan detects an existing session (no false-free), (c) asserts the launch path never calls `kill-server` (grep/trace). Proof-lever: red = current dotted names produce a false-free scan / kill-server reachable; green = incremented dot-free names + scoped kill only.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)

[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.result: Dot-free tmux session naming (hostname_cc_N) across superclaude, rescueclaude, constants.py, cc_worker_listener.py, stop_cc_minions.py. tmux mangles dots -> '_', which broke the free-slot scan, in-place equality, and reached kill-server. comms:// bus addresses stay dotted (separate namespace). PROVEN: hermetic red->green (dotted code fails the detect/find tests; underscore passes).
